title: helleborus viridis subsp. viridis
intro: Helleborus viridis subsp. viridis, also known as the green hellebore, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating rheumatism, as it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving anxiety and stress, and promoting wound healing. Hellebore preparations, including tinctures and infusions, have been used in herbal medicine to harness its medicinal properties, although caution is advised due to its potential toxicity.
